page: Amazon Web Services c5n.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 36 physical cores and 72 vCPUs running at 3.4 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 2.67 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage. It features a high-speed baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c5n.18xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance, scoring 66660.83 in stress-ng multi-core tests and 65810.62 in PassMark CPU extended instructions. Memory read bandwidth is also strong, reaching 3704.68 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 208.32 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, write bandwidth for large blocks is average at 90.55 GB/sec. The instance shows average performance in single-core tasks, web serving, and database operations, while text generation for LLM inference is weak, registering 7.23 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

The qualitative cost profile of this instance is shaped by its high network throughput and compute density, making it efficient for network-bound distributed applications. The lack of local storage means users must factor in external EBS costs, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. Given its 100 Gbps network interface and strong multi-core capabilities, the c5n.18xlarge is highly suited for high-performance computing, network appliances, distributed database operations, and intensive multi-threaded batch processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8124M processor. It delivers 72 vCPUs across 36 physical cores at 3.4 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. This instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but features a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory read bandwidth, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds remain average to weak. The qualitative cost efficiency of the c5n.18xlarge is optimized for high-throughput network tasks and distributed computing, making it a strong fit for network-intensive workloads, multi-threaded batch processing, and database operations that rely on network-attached storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 72 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, 100 Gbps network bandwidth, and strong multi-core performance for network-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5n.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 72 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-bandwidth, compute-optimized virtual server featuring seventy-two vCPUs and a baseline network speed of one hundred gigabits per second.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 72 vCPUs and 36 physical cores
192 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read bandwidth performance
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput applications
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Best suited for network-intensive workloads, HPC, and distributed databases
